parsed.answer: Part of Argentina is not in England. You may be thinking of the Falkland Islands (Islas Malvinas), which are a British Overseas Territory in the South Atlantic Ocean claimed by Argentina. The UK has administered them since 1833, and Argentina disputes this sovereignty. They were the subject of the 1982 Falklands War. The islands are near Argentina geographically but are governed by the UK, not part of England itself.
